summary: 7W / 7L across 14 gradeable matchups (50% hit rate); 2 no-picks (LAD@NYY postponed, PIT@CLE G2 pending SP data). Zero P&L exposure: all picks at SKIP tier or model-GOLDs capped to zero by hard caps. Claude's DOWNGRADE of SD@KC GOLD was correct (SD lost 6-1). HARD CAP 7 produced its third consecutive false negative in the sub-0.02 bullpen-gap band (MIA@MIL pre-cap score=5 grade-A, MIL won 8-6), crossing from monitoring-warranted to actionable. BAL beat model-favored HOU for the second straight day on the same opposing-signal shape; CIN@COL generated consecutive lopsided blowouts against the dual-opposing SKIP signal on opposite sides — both patterns demand scrutiny before next meeting.

PIT @ CLE (G1).headline: PIT blew out CLE 7-1; CONFIRM SKIP correct, and Stage 2 full-game direction (PIT) beat Stage 1 F5 lean (CLE 75.65%) at the slate's largest delta.
PIT @ CLE (G1).hypothesis: f5_full_delta=0.2691 is the highest on this slate; Stage 1 assigned CLE 75.65% F5 while Stage 2 flipped to PIT 51.3% full-game. PIT's 7-1 win is consistent with the Stage-2-reliable-at-high-delta archive pattern (now 5-of-6 tracked), reinforcing that the full-game model outperforms F5 probability at large deltas even without a stake. Edge +2.46pp below the 4pp floor and HARD CAP 10 correctly kept stake at zero — directional WIN with no P&L impact.
PIT @ CLE (G1).signals_to_recheck: f5_full_delta — Stage 2 full-game direction correct again at high delta (0.27); Stage 2 directional reliability at |delta|>0.12 continues to hold when PQI is not strongly opposing.

MIN @ CHC.headline: CHC beat MIN 6-2; D-grade dual-opposing SKIP was correctly sized at zero, but the opposing-signal direction was wrong again.
MIN @ CHC.hypothesis: Taj Bradley's 1.08 xERA edge and PQI -10.8 opposed CHC yet CHC won 6-2 — the opposing signals called the wrong winner, the same pair that correctly predicted MIN yesterday (7/17 MIN won 5-2). The stake-zeroing read was correct both days (D-grade near-coin-flip), but the dual-opposing SP+PQI configuration was directionally wrong today. Treat SP+PQI opposition as a reliable stake-suppression trigger, not a directional lean, when the matchup is at the margin (pick_prob near 0.50).
MIN @ CHC.signals_to_recheck: F1_xera_gap (opposing pick), pqi_diff — correctly triggered SKIP both days in this series but directional accuracy was 1-for-2 across 7/17 and 7/18; opposition signals on near-coinflip games should not be read as directional signals.

CHW @ TOR.headline: TOR edged CHW 1-0; CONFIRM SKIP correct on a two-dimension stack that correctly failed to meet the CAP 10 rescue bar.
CHW @ TOR.hypothesis: PQI +8.5 and Stage agree delta=0.03 both supported TOR but no F-signal fired, so HARD CAP 10's four-dimension rescue requirement went unmet and stake stayed at zero. TOR's 1-run win reflects genuine but insufficiently-corroborated conviction on a near-coin-flip (pick_prob=0.530). Directional WIN, zero P&L impact, no process failure.
CHW @ TOR.signals_to_recheck: None — HARD CAP 10 two-dimension-insufficient rule held as designed; 1-run win margin does not argue for lowering the four-dimension rescue bar.

CIN @ COL.headline: COL routed CIN 10-3; D-grade dual-opposing SKIP correctly sized at zero but missed a 7-run blowout for the second straight day on this matchup.
CIN @ COL.hypothesis: Rhett Lowder's 1.59 xERA edge and PQI -9.8 opposed COL — same dual-opposing structure that correctly suppressed a stake — but COL dominated by 7 runs. Yesterday (7/17), CIN was the pick, SP+PQI also opposed the pick, and CIN still won 7-2. Two consecutive matchday blowouts on opposite sides of the same team pair, both against the dual-opposing SKIP signal, is a stronger false-negative signal than either day alone: the SP-xERA and PQI features may be systematically misfiring for this pairing, possibly due to uncaptured Coors park factors or a structural SP-vs-lineup mismatch.
CIN @ COL.signals_to_recheck: edge_pp below 4pp floor, F1_xera_gap/pqi_diff opposing pick — CIN@COL has now produced two consecutive 7+ run blowouts against the dual-opposing SKIP signal on opposite pick sides (7/17: CIN won 7-2, 7/18: COL won 10-3); inspect Coors park factor interaction before the next CIN@COL game.

NYM @ PHI.headline: PHI beat NYM 6-1; SKIP correctly avoided a hallucination-zone pick that badly missed.
NYM @ PHI.hypothesis: fair_prob=0.373 (below the 0.42 viability floor), Luzardo's 1.26 xERA advantage over Manaea, PQI +25.4 opposing NYM, and edge 16.46pp above the 15pp ceiling all flagged a calibration-hallucination case. PHI's 6-1 win validates every opposing gate cleanly — a process success, not a variance failure. Correct stakes: zero.
NYM @ PHI.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob < 0.42 + edge_pp > 15pp ceiling — both gates fired and both proved correct together; the dual-gate hallucination-zone SKIP continues to perform as designed.

BAL @ HOU.headline: BAL beat HOU 4-2 — second consecutive day BAL won against a model-favored HOU in the same series.
BAL @ HOU.hypothesis: Trevor Rogers' 0.93 xERA edge and PQI -12.6 opposed HOU, with pick_prob=0.644 at the home-favorite scrutiny boundary (65%). Yesterday (7/17) the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM in this exact matchup lost 3-2; today D-grade dual-opposing signals correctly kept stake at zero and BAL won again. Two consecutive BAL wins over a model-favored HOU with the same SP+PQI opposing-signal shape (7/17 staked loss + 7/18 correctly-SKIPped loss) is a developing matchup-level caution worth tracking for a third instance. Zero net P&L impact so far due to 7/18 SKIP.
BAL @ HOU.signals_to_recheck: Home-favorite >65% scrutiny boundary — HOU has lost as the model favorite in this series two straight days (7/17 GOLD CONFIRM loss, 7/18 correctly-SKIPped); a third HOU-favored instance with same opposing shape should be treated as a standing blind spot.

SD @ KC.headline: KC blew out SD 6-1; DOWNGRADE of the GOLD SD pick was correct and protective.
SD @ KC.hypothesis: F3_swing_take_gap=613.5 fell well below the 1000 meaningful-anchor threshold; edge +0.16pp was trivial and outside the [4,15]pp valid window; HARD CAP 10 fired with no qualifying F-signal. Claude's DOWNGRADE to SKIP avoided what would have been a significant GOLD-tier loss — SD lost by 5 runs. A clean process success for the F3-threshold rule and the DOWNGRADE decision.
SD @ KC.signals_to_recheck: F3_swing_take_gap < 1000 — again correctly treated as a non-anchor; SD's 5-run loss reinforces that sub-1000 F3 should not elevate a marginal-edge pick to GOLD tier.

STL @ ARI.headline: ARI beat STL 5-3; CONFIRM SKIP correct, and ARI's win is consistent with the refined Stage 2 + PQI interaction rule from 7/17.
STL @ ARI.hypothesis: Dustin May's 0.68 xERA edge and PQI -6.5 opposed ARI, plus negative edge (-0.34pp, HARD CAP 1). Yesterday's STL@ARI established that Stage 2 high-delta direction degrades only when |pqi_diff| > 20 opposing; today's PQI opposition is 6.5 — well below that threshold — and ARI won, consistent with the refined rule. Directional WIN, no P&L impact, no process failure.
STL @ ARI.signals_to_recheck: pqi_diff magnitude threshold (>20 vs opposing Stage 2 direction) — ARI winning with small-opposition PQI (6.5) reaffirms the 7/17 refinement: only large PQI opposition degrades Stage 2 directional reliability.

TB @ BOS.headline: BOS edged TB 7-6; sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO and negative edge correctly kept this at zero stake on a genuinely close game.
TB @ BOS.hypothesis: Patrick Sandoval's 65-Statcast-pitch sample triggered sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO, and edge -1.21pp independently triggered HARD CAP 1. BOS's 1-run win reflects the game-level uncertainty the thin-SP-data veto is designed to avoid staking on. Directional WIN, zero P&L impact, no process failure.
TB @ BOS.signals_to_recheck: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO — correctly identified an unstakeable thin-sample SP game; 1-run margin confirms the uncertainty the gate was protecting against.

TEX @ ATL.headline: TEX beat ATL 7-6; sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO on Owen Murphy's thin sample correctly prevented a stake on a pick that lost.
TEX @ ATL.hypothesis: Owen Murphy's 67-Statcast-pitch sample triggered sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO on ATL's own starter; edge -1.03pp triggered HARD CAP 1 (pre-cap score dropped 3→1→0). Despite a positive pre-cap stack (PQI +5.2, Stage agree, F-signal; PRE_CAP_SCORE=3), the thin-SP veto and negative edge correctly zeroed the stake and TEX's 1-run win validates the caution — a game within 1 run matches a correctly-avoided coin-flip.
TEX @ ATL.signals_to_recheck: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO stacking with HARD CAP 1 — both fired on a game that finished within 1 run; the veto continues to earn its keep on close thin-sample-SP games regardless of whether the pick won or lost.

MIA @ MIL.headline: MIL beat MIA 8-6; a pre-cap score-5 grade-A GOLD stack was zeroed by HARD CAP 7 on a marginal bullpen gap — third false negative in the sub-0.02 band.
MIA @ MIL.hypothesis: F3=3225.7, F5=+0.86, SP xERA agreeing, and PQI +7.2 produced pre-cap score 5 (grade A), but HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185 — a gap the 7/17 postgame already flagged as sitting in the same problematic sub-0.02 range as two prior KC@BAL false negatives (7/10 +0.018, 7/12 +0.0189, both BAL won). MIL winning makes this the third consecutive false negative in that exact gap band. This crosses from 'monitoring warranted' to an actionable process issue: CAP 7's >=0 trigger is too tight and is suppressing genuine wins on marginal bullpen gaps that don't distinguish from noise at the 0.02 level.
MIA @ MIL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage — third false negative in the sub-0.02 hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap band (KC@BAL 7/10 +0.018, 7/12 +0.0189, MIA@MIL 7/18 +0.0185); recommend raising the CAP 7 trigger from >=0 to >=0.02 so marginal positive gaps don't zero out strong multi-signal stacks.

SF @ SEA.headline: SEA beat SF 4-3; SKIP correct, and the loss confirms yesterday's 7-0 SF win was pure variance as flagged.
SF @ SEA.hypothesis: Bryan Woo's 0.45 xERA edge and PQI +7.1 opposed SF — the same signal configuration that failed to prevent yesterday's 7-0 SF win (7/17), explicitly flagged 'do not extract a pattern from this win.' Today the expected directional result materialized (SF lost), reasserting the underlying signal's validity and confirming the prior blowout was noise. No process failure either day; SKIP was correct both times.
SF @ SEA.signals_to_recheck: None — 7/18 result reasserts the original SP-xERA/PQI read after 7/17's explicitly-flagged variance outlier; no signal recalibration needed.

LAD @ NYY.headline: Game postponed; no outcome to grade.
LAD @ NYY.hypothesis: Claude DOWNGRADEd NYY GOLD to SKIP on F5-only signal support (F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98) without F1 or F3 corroboration, plus SP-xERA and PQI -11.0 opposing NYY, consistent with the 7/17 F5-only-insufficient rule validated that same day (this exact matchup: NYY lost 2-1 after an F1*-driven DOWNGRADE). No result available — game postponed.
LAD @ NYY.signals_to_recheck: F5_bullpen_gap alone without F1/F3 — unresolved today; filed for future tracking when this game is rescheduled.

WSH @ OAK.headline: OAK routed WSH 15-1 — a complete reversal of yesterday's 23-4 WSH blowout in the same head-to-head.
WSH @ OAK.hypothesis: J.T. Ginn's 2.32 xERA edge, PQI +34.8 (slate's largest PQI opposition), and edge 16.08pp above the 15pp ceiling all opposed WSH, forming a much stronger opposition signature than yesterday's WSH@OAK (7/17: WSH won 23-4 despite hallucination-zone flag). Today's result validates the stronger opposing signals cleanly. Two consecutive 14+ run blowouts in opposite directions for the same pair within 24 hours — SKIP correctly avoided both extremes.
WSH @ OAK.signals_to_recheck: fair_prob/edge_pp ceiling + PQI opposition — correctly caught a 14-run loss the day after a 19-run win for the same team pair; do not treat yesterday's WSH blowout result as a prior for this matchup given the signal reversal.

DET @ LAA.headline: DET shut out LAA 7-0; the slate's most extreme multi-signal opposition (5 dimensions) predicted the loser correctly.
DET @ LAA.hypothesis: Tarik Skubal's 3.78 xERA advantage (slate's largest SP gap), PQI -38.3 (slate's largest PQI opposition), fair_prob=0.336 (below the 0.42 viability floor), Stage 1 giving LAA only 19.25% F5 probability, and grade D score=-4 all opposed LAA. DET's shutout validates every opposing signal at the extreme end. The 7/8 LAA@TEX outlier (13-1, extreme opposition failed) remains the exception; today reaffirms that extreme multi-signal opposition is directionally reliable in the majority of cases.
DET @ LAA.signals_to_recheck: Five-dimension signal opposition — correctly predicted the loser again; extreme multi-signal stacks remain the archive's most reliable directional (not stake-sizing) signals even without a qualifying tier.

PIT @ CLE (G2).headline: CLE won game 2 5-3; no pick was made since PIT's probable SP wasn't announced in time.
PIT @ CLE (G2).hypothesis: Pipeline correctly withheld a directional call pending the 100+ Statcast pitch minimum for PIT's game-2 starter. No evaluation is possible; CLE's win is unscored against any model output. The gate functioned as designed.
PIT @ CLE (G2).signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA non-directional hold — no signal to recheck; gate functioned as designed.

patterns_observed: [HARD CAP 7] pick-side bullpen disadvantage has now false-negatived three consecutive times in the sub-0.02 hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap band (KC@BAL 7/10 +0.018 BAL won 5-3, 7/12 +0.0189 BAL won 8-2, MIA@MIL 7/18 +0.0185 MIL won 8-6 on a pre-cap score-5/grade-A stack). This crosses from monitoring-warranted to actionable: raise CAP 7's trigger threshold from >=0 to >=0.02 (or apply the same |gap|>0.04 meaningful threshold already used for hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap elsewhere) so a marginal positive gap doesn't zero out a strong multi-signal stack.

BAL has beaten a model-favored HOU on back-to-back days in the same series (7/17: 3-2 as the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM loss; 7/18: 4-2 as a correctly-SKIPped D-grade CONFIRM), both with Trevor Rogers' SP-xERA edge and PQI opposing HOU. Zero net P&L impact since 7/18 was SKIPped, but if a third HOU-favorite game in this series presents the same opposing-signal shape (SP-xERA against + PQI against), treat it as a standing matchup-level caution rather than isolated noise.

CIN@COL has produced two consecutive lopsided blowouts (7/17: CIN won 7-2; 7/18: COL won 10-3) against the D-grade dual-opposing SP+PQI SKIP signal on opposite sides of the same matchup. Zero-stake sizing was correct both days, but the SP-xERA + PQI opposition called the wrong winner twice running on this pairing — inspect Coors park-factor interaction and the SP-vs-lineup matchup structure for CIN@COL before the next meeting, as the signal may be systematically misfiring on this team pair.

WSH@OAK swung from a 23-4 WSH blowout (7/17) to a 15-1 OAK blowout (7/18) on consecutive days, with the model picking WSH both times and SKIP correctly avoiding exposure to both extremes. Do not use one day's WSH@OAK result as a prior for the next game in this series; the matchup is running extreme-variance and directional accuracy depends entirely on which signal configuration is stronger on the day, not on recent results.
